question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables into the pinger container (e.g., via a compromised Kubernetes secret or ConfigMap) set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a very large value (e.g., '24h'), causing the ticker to fire only once per day and effectively preventing continuous transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to '0', causing submitWithRetry() to make only a single attempt per tick with no retries, so that any transient network error permanently drops that tick's transaction and prevents its gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to '1ms', causing every HTTP request in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to time out before receiving a response, exhausting all mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries and preventing the SDK client from initializing, which stops all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to '0', causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to make only a single HTTP attempt to the mirror REST endpoint, so that any single transient failure prevents client initialization and stops all gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a negative value (e.g., '-1'), which passes the 'cfg.amountTinybar == 0' check but causes AddHbarTransfer to reverse the transfer direction, potentially causing the transaction to be rejected by the network and never gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to a malformed account ID string, causing hiero.AccountIDFromString(cfg.toAccountID) in submitWithRetry() to return an error on every tick, permanently preventing all transaction gossip without crashing the process?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an attacker-controlled URL, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to fetch a crafted nodesEnvelope that routes all TransferTransaction gossip to attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to an invalid duration string (e.g., 'invalid'), causing toDuration() to silently return 1s default, and then set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a high value, creating a scenario where retry storms consume all available time between ticks and no new transactions are gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables provide an unparseable duration string for HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL, causing toDuration() to silently return 1*time.Second instead of failing, potentially overriding an intended longer interval and causing transaction flooding that results in network-level rate limiting and gossip suppression?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence cfg.network to an unrecognized value (not testnet/previewnet/mainnet/other) cause newClient() to return 'unknown network' error, preventing client initialization and stopping all transaction gossip permanently?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS certificate validation on the HTTP client used in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to perform a TLS MITM attack, presenting a forged certificate for the mirror REST endpoint and injecting a crafted nodesEnvelope that routes all transaction gossip to attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a node in the network map cause cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to return a non-nil error that is not context.Canceled, triggering the retry loop, and then cause every subsequent attempt to also fail, exhausting all retries and logging 'all attempts failed' — silently dropping the transaction without gossiping it?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-buffered ticker channel in the main select loop — where a slow submitWithRetry() call (due to retries and backoff) causes subsequent ticker ticks to be dropped — to create a sustained gossip gap where transactions are submitted far less frequently than cfg.interval, effectively suppressing gossip during network disruptions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user return a nodesEnvelope where all nodeEntry.ServiceEndpoints point to the same single node that the attacker controls, causing the SDK to have only one gossip target, and then take that node offline to prevent all transaction gossip with no fallback?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cfg.mirrorRest is taken from HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST without URL scheme validation to inject an SSRF payload (e.g., 'http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/') as the mirror REST URL, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to fetch cloud metadata instead of node data, failing to build a valid network map and preventing all gossip?
